common: CRITICAL — SCROLLING: Do NOT use keyboard End, Home, Page Down, or arrow keys to scroll any page. These keys can activate focused elements and navigate away from the current page. Always use window.scrollBy(0, window.innerHeight * 3) executed via browser JavaScript evaluation.

CRITICAL — PAGE NAVIGATION GUARD: After each scroll pass, verify the current page URL is still the expected feed/page. If the browser navigated to a detail page, navigate back to the feed URL before continuing extraction.

CRITICAL — TAB MANAGEMENT: The shared browser may have stale tabs from previous agents. At the start of each source step, verify you are on the correct tab and URL. If needed, navigate to the target URL in the current tab rather than opening new tabs. Do not close other tabs.
For home and subscriptions, the per-kind scroll instructions define the minimum scroll passes. Complete all required scroll passes before evaluating whether content is repeating.
Prefer the canonical watch URL, rendered title, rendered channel identity, rendered publish label, and rendered thumbnail from the current page before opening details.

CRITICAL — IN-PAGE ACCUMULATION: YouTube re-renders and removes cards while scrolling. Do NOT scroll first and only read the final visible DOM. For home and subscriptions, use a SINGLE async browser_evaluate call that creates an in-page Map keyed by videoId, extracts visible cards, scrolls, waits, re-extracts, and merges fields into that same Map across every pass before returning.

CRITICAL — DEDUP KEY: Deduplicate by canonical videoId only. Derive videoId from the visible watch link (`/watch?v=...`) or equivalent YouTube URL, then canonicalize to `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=<videoId>`. Do not deduplicate by title, channel name, or card text.

CRITICAL — URL CLEANUP: When a visible watch link includes extra query params such as `list`, `index`, `pp`, `start_radio`, or tracking params, keep those only for source notes if needed. The stored `canonicalUrl` should be the stripped watch URL built from videoId alone.

CRITICAL — FIELD MERGE RULE: When a later pass sees a video already in the Map, merge only newly discovered non-null fields into the existing record. Do not overwrite a populated field with null, empty text, or a shorter fallback string.

CRITICAL — ELIGIBLE CARD RULE: A candidate card only counts when it exposes a canonical watch URL and videoId. Shelves, Shorts trays, promoted cards, avatar-only modules, and channel recommendations do NOT count as extracted videos unless that individual card has its own `/watch?v=` link.

CRITICAL — SHORTS FILTER: Ignore links whose canonical target is `youtube.com/shorts/...` unless the card also exposes a normal watch URL for the same item. The subscription and home thresholds are about full video cards, not Shorts shelf units.

CRITICAL — PRESERVE SOURCE LABELS: Keep the rendered publish label exactly as shown on the page. Store it as both `publishLabel` and `publishDateText`. Preserve live/upcoming wording such as `LIVE`, `Streamed 54 minutes ago`, or `Scheduled for ...` rather than normalizing it away in the browser step.

CRITICAL — PUBLISHED TIMESTAMP: Before submitting a cache row, resolve `publishedAtMs` as an integer epoch-ms timestamp when you can compute it from `publishLabel` / `publishDateText` (including relative phrases such as `2 days ago`, `13 hours ago`, `just now`, and absolute dates such as `April 23`). When the source page exposes a machine-readable `datePublished` value from YouTube schema.org metadata or video JSON, use that timestamp directly. If every signal fails, omit `publishedAtMs` / leave it null rather than guessing, and note the affected row in `cycleSummary` so reflection can flag systemic gaps. Reject any computed `publishedAtMs` that is in the future beyond a few minutes of clock skew.

CRITICAL — CAPTURE SHAPE: Each extracted video object should include at minimum `videoId`, `canonicalUrl`, `title`, `channelName`, `channelUrl`, `channelHandle`, `channel` ({ name, url, handle }), `publishLabel`, `publishDateText`, `publishedAtMs`, `thumbnailUrl`, `duration`, `viewCount`, `viewCountLabel`, and `sourceKind`. Include null for fields you cannot read quickly.

CRITICAL — MINIMUM VALID RECORD: Do not count a card toward `uniqueVideos` unless it has a non-empty `videoId`, `canonicalUrl`, and `title`. Missing publish label or thumbnail should be preserved as null, but missing id/url/title means the card was not extracted successfully.

CRITICAL — ORDERING: Preserve first-seen order from the scroll loop. Add a temporary `firstSeenPass` or similar internal field while collecting, then sort the final `videos` array by that order before returning.

CRITICAL — INTERSTITIAL HANDLING: If YouTube shows consent, age-gate, sign-in, or other blocking/interstitial UI, record a short note in the returned `notes` array and keep extracting any still-visible watch cards. Only mark the source `ok=false` when the page is actually blocked from showing usable video cards.
CRITICAL — ZERO-GROWTH PASSES: If one scroll pass yields zero new videos, do NOT abandon the source. Finish the full mandatory scroll count, then report the low-yield behavior in `notes`.

CRITICAL — NO DETAIL-PAGE DETOURS: Do not open individual watch pages just to compensate for a weak card extractor during home/subscriptions. The goal of these steps is feed-card extraction, not per-video deep reads.
When a field is not readily visible from the rendered page, store null instead of spending extra time hunting for it.
If YouTube lands on consent, age-gate, sign-in, or other blocking pages, keep going where possible and mention the blocked source briefly in the final status.
For home and subscriptions, write the full capture object to `data/tmp/...capture` with browser_evaluate's filename support instead of sending the entire video batch through stdout.
Your final response for home and subscriptions must be the raw JSON capture object only. Do not wrap it in markdown or add explanatory prose.

home: MANDATORY PAGE RESET for Home: Navigate to `https://www.youtube.com/` in the current tab and wait for the main feed to render before extracting. Do not rely on a previously open tab that might still be on Watch, Shorts, or another shelf.

MANDATORY CARD ROOT for Home: `document.querySelectorAll('ytd-rich-item-renderer')` is the card container on Home. Accept only cards that expose a canonical `/watch?v=` anchor. Reject shelves (`ytd-reel-shelf-renderer`, `ytd-rich-shelf-renderer`), ads (`ytd-ad-slot-renderer`), promoted modules (`ytd-promoted-video-renderer`), and notification-only modules without a watch link.

MANDATORY VIDEO ID for Home: Per card, start from `card.querySelector('a[href^="/watch?v="]')?.getAttribute('href')`, then regex-extract `videoId` via `/[?&]v=([A-Za-z0-9_-]+)/`. The `a[href^="/watch?v="]` pattern is the stable YouTube watch-URL contract. Anchor all Home extraction on this, and build `canonicalUrl` as `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=<videoId>` after stripping `list`, `index`, `pp`, `start_radio`, and tracking params.

MANDATORY TITLE for Home: PRIMARY: `Array.from(card.querySelectorAll('a[aria-label]')).find(a => (a.getAttribute('aria-label') || '').length > 10)?.getAttribute('aria-label')`; strip any trailing spoken-duration phrase matching `\s*\d+\s*(hour|minute|second)s?(\s*,\s*\d+\s*(hour|minute|second)s?)*\s*$`. FALLBACK: the second line of `card.innerText.split('\n').map(s => s.trim()).filter(Boolean)`. Keep the fuller title when both resolve; if the innerText line is clipped with ellipsis but the aria-label is complete, use the aria-label.

MANDATORY CHANNEL for Home: PRIMARY: `card.querySelector('[aria-label^="Go to channel "]')?.getAttribute('aria-label')?.replace(/^Go to channel\s*/, '').trim()`. FALLBACK: the third line of the innerText layout. Channel URL and handle: `card.querySelector('a[href^="/@"]')?.getAttribute('href')` -> `channelUrl` after prepending `https://www.youtube.com`, and derive `channelHandle` from the `/@handle` path.

MANDATORY PUBLISH LABEL for Home: Parse `card.innerText.split('\n')`; keep the line that contains any of `ago`, `Streamed`, `Scheduled`, `Premiered`, `LIVE`, or a date pattern. Store that exact rendered text in both `publishLabel` and `publishDateText`. Do not normalize.

MANDATORY VIEW COUNT for Home: From the same `card.innerText.split('\n')` lines, find the line whose lowercased text contains `views` or `watching`. Store that exact rendered string verbatim in `viewCountLabel` (e.g., `1.2M views`, `12K views`, `1,234 views`, `342 watching`). Derive `viewCount` numerically: match the leading number plus optional comma group, then optional `K`/`M`/`B` suffix → multiply by 1e3/1e6/1e9; treat `1,234 views` as 1234 (strip commas, no suffix). When the line wording does not yield a parseable count (some live or upcoming cards may render `Premieres in ...` or other status text instead of a views/watching token), keep `viewCount` null while still preserving the raw text in `viewCountLabel` if any was found, otherwise both null. Some YouTube layouts split views and publish into one combined line such as `1.2M views • 2 days ago`; in that case extract the views token (first segment containing `views` or `watching`) for these fields and leave the publish-label rule untouched.

MANDATORY THUMBNAIL for Home: Iterate `card.querySelectorAll('img')` and keep the first image whose `src || currentSrc` starts with `https://i.ytimg.com/vi/`. Skip blank placeholders and data URIs. Lazy-loaded images outside the current scroll viewport may not have populated `src` yet; the mandatory scroll loop should bring them in.

MANDATORY DURATION for Home: PRIMARY: `card.querySelector('badge-shape .ytBadgeShapeText')?.innerText?.trim()` when the page-current thumbnail-badge structure is present. FALLBACK: the first line of the innerText layout. Live, upcoming, or premiere cards may legitimately have `null` duration; keep `null` rather than inventing a placeholder.

MANDATORY SELECTOR PRIORITY RULE for Home: Prefer aria-label and innerText-line parsing over class-name selectors whenever both can deliver the same field. Legacy selectors such as `#video-title-link`, `ytd-channel-name a`, `#channel-name a`, `ytd-video-meta-block #metadata-line span`, `ytd-thumbnail img`, and `ytd-thumbnail-overlay-time-status-renderer span` are secondary fallbacks only if the primary aria-label and text-layout paths both return null.

MANDATORY ADAPT-WHEN-ZERO ESCAPE CLAUSE for Home: If after the first pass the in-page Map has size 0 and the page visibly rendered content (`document.body.innerText.length > 1000` and `document.querySelectorAll('ytd-rich-item-renderer').length > 20`), the extraction path is broken. Do not silently report `uniqueVideos: 0` through the rest of the scroll loop. Inspect one card's `outerHTML.slice(0, 3000)`, all aria-labels, and the innerText line layout, then adapt and record the adapted strategy in `notes`. This clause applies only when the first pass returns literally zero.

MANDATORY VIRTUAL DOM + SCROLL LOOP for Home: In one async browser_evaluate call, create `const videos = new Map()` and `const notes = []`. Define helper functions for `pickText`, `absoluteUrl`, `canonicalWatchUrl`, `videoIdFromUrl`, `pickThumbnail`, and `mergeVideo(existing, fresh)`. Extract the initially visible Home cards into the Map before any scroll.

MANDATORY EXTRACTION LOOP for Home: On every pass, query the visible card roots again, compute `freshVideo` objects, and immediately `videos.set(videoId, mergeVideo(videos.get(videoId), freshVideo))`. Do not build separate per-pass arrays that you deduplicate later in scratchpad text.

MANDATORY SCROLL LOOP for Home: After the initial extraction, perform at least 3 additional scroll passes using `window.scrollBy(0, window.innerHeight * 3)` with a 3-second wait after each pass. After every pass, re-run the same extractor against currently visible cards and merge them into the same in-page Map. Do not stop early just because one pass produced zero new items.

MANDATORY COUNT RULE for Home: Do not mark Home covered until you have completed the required passes and counted at least 15 unique canonical videoIds, or until the required passes finish and the page truly failed to expose enough eligible watch cards. Low yield after the mandatory passes must be explained in `notes`, not silently accepted.

MANDATORY OUTPUT SHAPE for Home: Return an object shaped like `{ ok, sourceKind: "home", pageUrl, completedScrollPasses, requiredScrollPasses, uniqueVideos, notes, videos }`, where `videos` is an array produced from the Map values sorted by first-seen order. Each video object must include `publishedAtMs`, `viewCount`, and `viewCountLabel` alongside the other capture fields; these may be null when the card legitimately exposed no parseable publish timestamp or no views/watching label.
MANDATORY HOME NOTES: If Home visibly mixes non-video shelves, autoplay modules, or stale repeated cards, put that in `notes` while still returning the valid watch-card videos you did capture.

MANDATORY FILE OUTPUT for Home: Ensure `data/tmp` exists before saving. Use browser_evaluate's filename parameter to write the full capture object directly to `data/tmp/youtube-home-<run-id>.capture`.

MANDATORY RETURN for Home: After writing the capture file, keep the same raw JSON object available for your scratchpad and later candidate evaluation. Do not run persistence scripts or cache upserts from this prompt.
subscriptions: MANDATORY PAGE RESET for Subscriptions: Navigate to `https://www.youtube.com/feed/subscriptions` in the current tab and wait for the feed to render before extracting. Do not continue from a stale tab that may still be focused on an individual watch page.

MANDATORY CARD ROOT for Subscriptions: `document.querySelectorAll('ytd-rich-item-renderer')` is the card container on Subscriptions. Accept only cards that expose a canonical `/watch?v=` anchor. Reject shelves (`ytd-reel-shelf-renderer`, `ytd-rich-shelf-renderer`), ads (`ytd-ad-slot-renderer`), promoted modules (`ytd-promoted-video-renderer`), and notification-only modules without a watch link.

MANDATORY VIDEO ID for Subscriptions: Per card, start from `card.querySelector('a[href^="/watch?v="]')?.getAttribute('href')`, then regex-extract `videoId` via `/[?&]v=([A-Za-z0-9_-]+)/`. The `a[href^="/watch?v="]` pattern is the stable YouTube watch-URL contract. Anchor all Subscriptions extraction on this, and build `canonicalUrl` as `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=<videoId>` after stripping `list`, `index`, `pp`, `start_radio`, and tracking params.

MANDATORY TITLE for Subscriptions: PRIMARY: `Array.from(card.querySelectorAll('a[aria-label]')).find(a => (a.getAttribute('aria-label') || '').length > 10)?.getAttribute('aria-label')`; strip any trailing spoken-duration phrase matching `\s*\d+\s*(hour|minute|second)s?(\s*,\s*\d+\s*(hour|minute|second)s?)*\s*$`. FALLBACK: the second line of `card.innerText.split('\n').map(s => s.trim()).filter(Boolean)`. Keep the fuller title when both resolve; if the innerText line is clipped with ellipsis but the aria-label is complete, use the aria-label.

MANDATORY CHANNEL for Subscriptions: PRIMARY: `card.querySelector('[aria-label^="Go to channel "]')?.getAttribute('aria-label')?.replace(/^Go to channel\s*/, '').trim()`. FALLBACK: the third line of the innerText layout. Channel URL and handle: `card.querySelector('a[href^="/@"]')?.getAttribute('href')` -> `channelUrl` after prepending `https://www.youtube.com`, and derive `channelHandle` from the `/@handle` path.

MANDATORY PUBLISH LABEL for Subscriptions: Parse `card.innerText.split('\n')`; keep the line that contains any of `ago`, `Streamed`, `Scheduled`, `Premiered`, `LIVE`, or a date pattern. Store that exact rendered text in both `publishLabel` and `publishDateText`. Do not normalize.

MANDATORY VIEW COUNT for Subscriptions: From the same `card.innerText.split('\n')` lines, find the line whose lowercased text contains `views` or `watching`. Store that exact rendered string verbatim in `viewCountLabel` (e.g., `1.2M views`, `12K views`, `1,234 views`, `342 watching`). Derive `viewCount` numerically: match the leading number plus optional comma group, then optional `K`/`M`/`B` suffix → multiply by 1e3/1e6/1e9; treat `1,234 views` as 1234 (strip commas, no suffix). When the line wording does not yield a parseable count (some live or upcoming cards may render `Premieres in ...` or other status text instead of a views/watching token), keep `viewCount` null while still preserving the raw text in `viewCountLabel` if any was found, otherwise both null. Some YouTube layouts split views and publish into one combined line such as `1.2M views • 2 days ago`; in that case extract the views token (first segment containing `views` or `watching`) for these fields and leave the publish-label rule untouched.

MANDATORY THUMBNAIL for Subscriptions: Iterate `card.querySelectorAll('img')` and keep the first image whose `src || currentSrc` starts with `https://i.ytimg.com/vi/`. Skip blank placeholders and data URIs. Lazy-loaded images outside the current scroll viewport may not have populated `src` yet; the mandatory scroll loop should bring them in.

MANDATORY DURATION for Subscriptions: PRIMARY: `card.querySelector('badge-shape .ytBadgeShapeText')?.innerText?.trim()` when the page-current thumbnail-badge structure is present. FALLBACK: the first line of the innerText layout. Live, upcoming, or premiere cards may legitimately have `null` duration; keep `null` rather than inventing a placeholder.

MANDATORY SELECTOR PRIORITY RULE for Subscriptions: Prefer aria-label and innerText-line parsing over class-name selectors whenever both can deliver the same field. Legacy selectors such as `#video-title-link`, `ytd-channel-name a`, `#channel-name a`, `ytd-video-meta-block #metadata-line span`, `ytd-thumbnail img`, and `ytd-thumbnail-overlay-time-status-renderer span` are secondary fallbacks only if the primary aria-label and text-layout paths both return null.

MANDATORY ADAPT-WHEN-ZERO ESCAPE CLAUSE for Subscriptions: If after the first pass the in-page Map has size 0 and the page visibly rendered content (`document.body.innerText.length > 1000` and `document.querySelectorAll('ytd-rich-item-renderer').length > 20`), the extraction path is broken. Do not silently report `uniqueVideos: 0` through the rest of the scroll loop. Inspect one card's `outerHTML.slice(0, 3000)`, all aria-labels, and the innerText line layout, then adapt and record the adapted strategy in `notes`. This clause applies only when the first pass returns literally zero.

MANDATORY SUBSCRIPTIONS LIVE LABELS: If the card shows `LIVE`, `Upcoming`, `Premieres in`, or similar status text instead of a normal relative-age label, keep that exact rendered text in `publishLabel` and `publishDateText` rather than inventing a normalized timestamp in-browser.

MANDATORY VIRTUAL DOM + SCROLL LOOP for Subscriptions: In one async browser_evaluate call, create `const videos = new Map()` and `const notes = []`. Define helpers that canonicalize URLs, derive `videoId`, trim text, pick thumbnails, and merge records without losing previously captured values. Extract the initial visible subscription cards into the Map before the first scroll.

MANDATORY EXTRACTION LOOP for Subscriptions: On every pass, query the visible feed card roots again, compute `freshVideo` objects, and immediately merge them into the same `videos` Map. The browser step should accumulate state in-page; do not depend on the outer agent scratchpad to remember earlier cards.

MANDATORY LIVE-CARD HANDLING for Subscriptions: Live and premiere cards still count when they expose a canonical watch URL. Preserve their rendered labels exactly, set `publishLabel` and `publishDateText` from that label, and allow `duration` to be null when the card shows `LIVE`, `Upcoming`, or a scheduled banner instead of a duration chip.

MANDATORY SCROLL LOOP for Subscriptions: After the initial extraction, perform at least 5 additional scroll passes using `window.scrollBy(0, window.innerHeight * 3)` with 3-second waits after each pass. After every pass, re-run the same extractor against the currently visible feed cards and merge them into the same in-page Map. If a pass yields zero new cards, record that in `notes` and continue until all mandatory passes are complete.

MANDATORY COUNT RULE for Subscriptions: Do not move to channel or search steps until the mandatory passes are complete and you have either captured at least 20 unique canonical videoIds or you can explicitly explain in `notes` why the rendered page exposed fewer than 20 eligible watch cards despite the completed pass budget.

MANDATORY OUTPUT SHAPE for Subscriptions: Return an object shaped like `{ ok, sourceKind: "subscriptions", pageUrl, completedScrollPasses, requiredScrollPasses, uniqueVideos, notes, videos }`, where each video includes `videoId`, `canonicalUrl`, `title`, `channelName`, `channelUrl`, `channelHandle`, `channel`, `publishLabel`, `publishDateText`, `publishedAtMs`, `thumbnailUrl`, `duration`, `viewCount`, `viewCountLabel`, and `sourceKind: "subscriptions"`. `publishedAtMs`, `viewCount`, and `viewCountLabel` may be null when the card legitimately exposed no parseable publish timestamp or no views/watching label.

MANDATORY SUBSCRIPTIONS NOTES: If the page shows the user is signed out, filtered to a sparse subset, or repeatedly recycling the same few feed cards, note that explicitly in `notes` while still returning any valid videos captured.

MANDATORY FILE OUTPUT for Subscriptions: Ensure `data/tmp` exists before saving. Use browser_evaluate's filename parameter to write the full capture object directly to `data/tmp/youtube-subs-<run-id>.capture`.

MANDATORY RETURN for Subscriptions: After writing the capture file, keep the same raw JSON object available for your scratchpad and later candidate evaluation. Do not run persistence scripts or cache upserts from this prompt.
channel: Channel pages are targeted follow-ups from the current cycle's gaps and promising leads. Stay on the requested channel rather than substituting adjacent recommendations.
search: YouTube search may render different result card DOM than home or subscriptions. Treat extraction differences as page-reading work, not a reason to abandon the step.
